data.title: HSBC Repurchases Nearly 4 Million Shares
data.description:
HSBC bought back for cancelation a total of 3.8 million shares in the UK and Hong Kong on Friday under its new buy-back program, according to a Monday filing with the Hong Kong exchange.

The British lender repurchased 2,811,287 shares in the UK at a weighted average price of 8.84 pounds sterling apiece and 982,000 shares in Hong Kong at a weighted average price of HK$86.81 per share.

Since the start of the current buyback program, HSBC has bought back 3,793,287 shares totaling about $42.3 million.

data.title: UBS Downgrades Qube Holdings to Neutral from Buy; Price Target is AU$4.40
data.description: Qube Holdings has an average rating of outperform and price targets ranging from AU$3.70 to AU$4.92, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.title: Bell Potter Downgrades Domain Holdings Australia to Hold from Buy; Price Target is AU$4.20
data.description: Domain Holdings Australia has an average rating of hold and price targets ranging from AU$2.70 to AU$4.20, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.title: Societe Generale Puts Lubomira Rochet in Charge of French Retail Banking, Chief Operating Office
data.description:
By Elena Vardon

Societe Generale appointed Lubomira Rochet as its executive vice president to help Chief Executive Slawomir Krupa oversee French retail banking activities and private banking and insurance.

The French bank on Monday said that Rochet--who will join the group and its executive committee in April--will also assist Krupa in leading the chief operating office's activities, including technology, procurement and real estate.

Rochet was an independent director on Societe Generale's board between 2017 and 2024, and has been a partner at JAB Holding Co. since 2021.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: Alibaba to Spend More Than $52 Billion in AI, Cloud Over Next Three Years
data.description:
By Tracy Qu

Alibaba Group plans to invest more than $52 billion on AI and cloud infrastructure over the next three years, in a bid to seize more opportunities in the artificial-intelligence era.

The spending of at least 380 billion yuan, equivalent to $52.41 billion, will surpass the company's AI and cloud computing investment over the past decade, Alibaba said in a post Monday on its news site.

Alibaba first mentioned the plan last week when the company reported its results but didn't provide a specific figure.

The technology giant co-founded by Jack Ma delivered better-than-expected results for three months ended December, with revenue growth accelerating to its fastest pace since late 2023, supported by improvements in its e-commerce and cloud businesses.

Major Chinese tech companies, from Alibaba to Baidu, have been ramping up efforts to spur AI business growth as advancements by homegrown upstart DeepSeek have gained global attention. Alibaba co-founder Joe Tsai earlier this month said its AI technology would be integrated into Apple's iPhones for the Chinese market, a move that analysts expect to burnish the Hangzhou-based company's brand image and benefit its long-term growth. Last month, Alibaba introduced the Qwen2.5 Max, the latest version of its AI model, which it said was competitive with global leaders, including DeepSeek-V3.

Alibaba's pledged AI and cloud investment exceeded the forecast of Citi analysts, who projected spending of 350 billion yuan from the fiscal fourth quarter of 2025 through the end of fiscal 2028.

"We believe Alibaba is well-positioned to capture the influx demand of cloud computing services in the coming years," they said in a commentary.

Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com

data.title: Moelis & Company Upgrades Rural Funds Group to Buy from Hold; Price Target is AU$2.14
data.description: Rural Funds Group has an average rating of outperform and price targets ranging from AU$1.84 to AU$2.51, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.title: Macquarie Upgrades Jumbo Interactive to Outperform from Neutral; Price Target is AU$14.80
data.description: Jumbo Interactive has an average rating of outperform and price targets ranging from AU$11.60 to AU$17.85, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.title: Synergy Grid Unit NGCP Wins Arbitration Case
data.description:
Synergy Grid & Development Phils said the Singapore International Arbitration Centre ruled in favor of the National Grid of the Philippines (NGCP) in a case against the Power Sector Assets & Liabilities Management Corporation (PSALM) and the National Transmission Corporation (TRANSCO).

The tribunal upheld NGCP's compliance with nationality restrictions, validated its 57.88 billion Philippine pesos prepayment, and significantly reduced TRANSCO's claim to 372.77 million pesos, according to a Monday Philippine bourse filing.

NGCP will also receive 56.5 million pesos in indemnification, the filing said.

Synergy Grid & Development Phils is the holding company that owns a controlling stake in the NGCP.

data.title: German Defense Spending Set to Increase After Federal Election — Market Talk
data.description: Defense spending will likely dominate German coalition talks, analysts at Deutsche Bank write. It is highly likely that the CDU/CSU and SPD will agree on higher defense spending and it should rise to at least 2.5% of GDP over the next term, they add. A meaningful uptick in defense spending could come as early as 2026, they write. The tricky question will be how to finance the spending increase, especially as reform to the debt brake will be contentious and require political compromises, the analysts add. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: South Korea's Kospi Falls 0.35%, Dragged by Chip Stocks — Market Talk
data.description:
0731 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi fell 0.35% to close at 2645.27, dragged by semiconductor stocks. Wall Street's falls led by U.S. chip makers weighed on sentiment. Trading volume was thin amid caution ahead of the Bank of Korea's rate decision due Tuesday. Foreign and institutional investors were net sellers. Local memory-chip makers Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix declined 1.55% and 2.15%, respectively, tracking their U.S. peers' retreat. Chip-making equipment supplier Hanmi Semiconductor lost 4.72%. USD/KRW settled 0.5% higher at 1,427.40 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield was down 2.9 bps at 2.827%.(kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0730 GMT - Defense spending will likely dominate German coalition talks, analysts at Deutsche Bank write. It is highly likely that the CDU/CSU and SPD will agree on higher defense spending and it should rise to at least 2.5% of GDP over the next term, they add. A meaningful uptick in defense spending could come as early as 2026, they write. The tricky question will be how to finance the spending increase, especially as reform to the debt brake will be contentious and require political compromises, the analysts add. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0727 GMT - The euro and European stock futures rise on relief there were no major surprises from Germany's federal elections Sunday, Swissquote Bank's Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a note. The Conservative Christian Democrat Union won the election with 28.5% of the vote while the far-right Alternative for Germany were in second at 20.8%. "The kneejerk reaction is a swift rebound of the euro and the equity futures on hope of higher spending by the new German government would tackle the economic weakness of past years," Ozkardeskaya says. The euro rises 0.4% after hitting a four-week high of $1.0531 overnight, according to FactSet. IG futures data show Germany's DAX opening 0.95% higher and France's CAC up 0.1%, although the U.K.'s FTSE 100 is seen opening down 0.1%. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0726 GMT - German Bunds are expected to find support amid challenges in Germany's efforts to reform the debt brake, but are likely to consolidate this morning as risk sentiment stabilizes, says Commerzbank Research's Rainer Guntermann in a note. The major hurdle that the new government faces is the lack of a two-third majority with the Greens, making reforming the debt brake difficult, says the rates strategist. "And a special budget for defence will probably not be possible without major concessions to the Left, which is staunchy against more money for the military," he says. The Christian Democratic Union scored a solid win, allowing chancellor candidate Friedrich Merz to begin government formation talks. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0723 GMT - Germany's incoming administration must drive an economic turnaround and foster innovation to restore the country's competitiveness, the Federation of German Industries (BDI) says in a statement. Policymakers should prioritize economic growth to counteract declining industrial production and Germany's weakening position as a business hub, the industry group says. The BDI outlines four key priorities for the new government to focus on: enhancing competitiveness, promoting innovation, modernizing the state, and strengthening Germany's global standing. The group proposes lowering energy prices--which have risen since the start of Russia's invasion of Ukraine-- lower company taxes and a reduction to bureaucracy, among others.(helena.smolak@wsj.com)

0712 GMT - A two-party coalition between the conservative party CDU/CSU and Chancellor Olaf Scholz's Social Democratic Party is now the most likely outcome in Germany's federal election, analysts at Deutsche Bank write. Germany's corporate sector is expected to see a government led by a strong CDU/CSU as positive, they add. It should promise less policy gridlock and uncertainty than under the outgoing government, the analysts write. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0712 GMT - China may set the 2025 GDP growth target at 4.5%-5% and allow for some flexibility given external uncertainties, Nomura economists say in a research note. Domestic consumption could be a key focus for Beijing this year to counter external headwinds. Authorities are likely to expand existing trade-in programs and other subsidies, the economists say. China could raise its official fiscal deficit to 4% of GDP from 3% in 2024 and may announce record-high issuance of special government bonds to support investment and consumption, they say. Meanwhile, structural reform could accelerate this year, as Beijing places more emphasis on high-quality growth and support for the private sector, they say. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0703 GMT - The German election results make a two-party government between the conservative CDU/CSU and the Social Democrats (SPD) the most likely result, which is a positive outcome for the German economy, says Danske Bank Research. Friedrich Merz is almost certain to become the next chancellor as his Christian Democratic Party, along with its Bavarian sister party CSU, emerged as the most voted in Sunday's elections. A majority government with the Social Democrats is possible, says Danske's Oscar Christian Dahl Pedersen, because two parties, the Free Democrats and the BSW fell below the 5% threshold to enter Parliament. "A two-party 'Grand coalition' government is a positive outcome because decision making is easier than in a three-party government." (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0701 GMT - The Bank of Thailand is likely to leave its policy rate unchanged at 2.25% on Wednesday, Nomura economists say. They expect the tone of the policy statement to turn dovish, paving the way for cuts ahead. "The BOT may still argue that cuts are less effective when uncertainty is high, so the preservation of policy space is deemed more important, for now," say Charnon Boonnuch and Euben Paracuelles in a note. Nomura expects the central bank's policy statement to flag downside risks to its GDP growth forecasts but without releasing new economic projections. Nomura maintains its forecast for a 25bp cut in April followed by another 25bp cut in August. It also thinks inflation will undershoot the BOT's 1%-3% target range by March before turning negative in 2Q.(monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0647 GMT - Higher bond supply to fund defence spending presents bearish risk for eurozone duration, says Citi Research's Jamie Searle in a note. While higher supply could slow a decline in bond yields, it is unlikely to prevent it, he says. Therefore, Citi Research continues to expect 10-year Bund yields to track back to the lower half of the 2%-2.5% range in the coming weeks or months. However, there is an emerging risk that this return to lower yield levels might take longer than thought, given issuance pressure and the possible compromise of a slowing to quarterly rate cuts by the European Central Bank before trough rate is reached, he says. The 10-year Bund yield closed at 2.47% on Friday, according to Tradeweb.(emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0643 GMT - The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield could move lower in the coming months, alongside anticipated rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, says Mediolanum International Funds Ltd's Marco Meijer. "​In the near term, we expect 10-year U.S. Treasury yields to fall toward 4% by the summer, as uncertainty around tariffs has dampened the U.S. growth outlook," the deputy head of fixed income says. At the same time, some progress in reducing the budget deficit could further support a downward move in yields, he says. Mediolanum expects the Fed to deliver three rate cuts of 25 basis points each this year but acknowledges that the risks are skewed towards fewer rate cuts. The 10-year Treasury yield is up 1 basis point to 4.430%, according to LSEG. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0612 GMT - China will likely to set 2025's economic growth target around 5%, BofA Securities analysts write in a note. This year's GDP growth target will deliver few surprises, despite the risks from tariffs, they add. The national GDP growth target is usually in line with provincial targets released earlier. The three largest provinces in China--Guangdong, Shandong and Jiangsu--have all maintained their growth target at the same level as last year's, suggesting the national GDP target should stay the same, they say. Beijing will likely hint at further lowering of interest rates and banks' reserve requirement ratio, they add. The policymakers may set a more aggressive budget deficit of at least 3.8% to shore up fiscal stimulus efforts, they say. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0731 GMT - A decisive fiscal regime shift in Germany seems unlikely after centrist parties failed to win a constitutional majority, analysts at Deutsche Bank write. Even with the support of the green party, the conservative party CDU/CSU and Chancellor Olaf Scholz's Social Democratic Party jointly hold just under 66% of seats, they add. Constitutional changes, including to the country's fiscal rules, known as debt brake, would rely on the support of far-left Die Linke or far-right AfD, the analysts write. This could be seen as negative by investors as its reduces the likelihood of a decisive shift in Germany's fiscal framework, the analysts add. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0727 GMT - The euro and European stock futures rise on relief there were no major surprises from Germany's federal elections Sunday, Swissquote Bank's Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a note. The Conservative Christian Democrat Union won the election with 28.5% of the vote while the far-right Alternative for Germany were in second at 20.8%. "The kneejerk reaction is a swift rebound of the euro and the equity futures on hope of higher spending by the new German government would tackle the economic weakness of past years," Ozkardeskaya says. The euro rises 0.4% after hitting a four-week high of $1.0531 overnight, according to FactSet. IG futures data show Germany's DAX opening 0.95% higher and France's CAC up 0.1%, although the U.K.'s FTSE 100 is seen opening down 0.1%. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0724 GMT - Chinese shares closed mixed, with the Shanghai Composite Index dropping 0.2% to 3373.03. The Shenzhen Composite edged 0.1% higher, while the ChiNext Price Index was 0.7% lower. Investors are awaiting the upcoming Two Sessions meetings for more policy signals from Beijing. Among major stocks, property stocks advanced. Poly Developments & Holdings was 1.8% higher and China Merchants Shekou Industrial Zone Holdings rose 3.4%. Decliners included Hygon Information Technology, which was 1.85% lower, and BYD, down 1.75%. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0723 GMT - Germany's incoming administration must drive an economic turnaround and foster innovation to restore the country's competitiveness, the Federation of German Industries (BDI) says in a statement. Policymakers should prioritize economic growth to counteract declining industrial production and Germany's weakening position as a business hub, the industry group says. The BDI outlines four key priorities for the new government to focus on: enhancing competitiveness, promoting innovation, modernizing the state, and strengthening Germany's global standing. The group proposes lowering energy prices--which have risen since the start of Russia's invasion of Ukraine-- lower company taxes and a reduction to bureaucracy, among others.(helena.smolak@wsj.com)

0718 GMT - Lenovo's PC volumes should stabilize in calendar 2025, supported by an accelerated Windows 11 replacement cycle, CGS International analyst Ray Kwok says in a note. He projects Lenovo's PC revenue to rise 1% and 3% for FY 2025 and FY 2026, respectively, as average selling prices rise due to better product mix and bigger contributions by premium PCs, such as gaming PCs and AI PCs. CGS Interational raises its FY 2026 and FY 2027 EPS forecasts on Lenovo by 13%-15% as the global PC market recovery drives demand for AI PCs and servers, supporting profitability. The brokerage raises the stock's target to HK$15.00 from HK$13.30 and maintains an add rating. Shares are last at HK$13.02. (hoishan.chan@wsj.com)

0713 GMT - There needs to be more clarity regarding the focus sectors of Singapore's S$5 billion Equity Market Development Program, says Shekhar Jaiswal, head of equity research at RHB Singapore, in a note. This would ensure the program aligns with sectors that make up the largest portion of the potential initial public offering pipeline for the Singapore Exchange. Jaiswal also stresses the need for greater transparency on how much of the funds will be allocated to small- and mid-cap companies. "Without this clarity, there remains a risk that much of the funds may get allocated to large-cap/or index stocks," he adds.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0712 GMT - A two-party coalition between the conservative party CDU/CSU and Chancellor Olaf Scholz's Social Democratic Party is now the most likely outcome in Germany's federal election, analysts at Deutsche Bank write. Germany's corporate sector is expected to see a government led by a strong CDU/CSU as positive, they add. It should promise less policy gridlock and uncertainty than under the outgoing government, the analysts write. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0703 GMT - Federal Bank's key priorities over the medium-term may better monetize its balance-sheet strengths, based its analysts' meeting, HDFC Securities analysts say in a research report. The bank highlighted priorities including improved mobilization of current and savings accounts reflecting in lower cost of funds and scaling its medium-yield businesses to drive stronger risk-adjusted returns, the analysts note. The Indian bank has a strategic focus on profitability, but it is likely to undergo an extended investment phase which could push operating-leverage benefits more toward FY 2027. The brokerage trims the stock's target price to INR210.00 from INR220.00, with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 1.5% higher at INR182.40. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0633 GMT - StarHub's earnings recovery may be delayed, according to CGS International analysts in a research report. They highlight factors such as intense mobile competition, which is affecting StarHub's average-revenue-per-user growth, and the slower realization of the benefits from its 'DARE+' strategy. Management notes that Singapore's mobile market remains "hypercompetitive" and plans to compete aggressively in 2025 to defend and grow market share. The analysts believe the telecom conglomerate's 2025 earnings outlook is uncertain. The brokerage lowers its 2025-2026 core EPS estimates for StarHub by 11%-17%. CGS International downgrades the stock to hold from add and lowers the stock's target price to S$1.30 from S$1.40. Shares are down 0.8% at S$1.23. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0633 GMT - Singapore's new set of measures to boost the equities market demonstrate a strong governmental commitment to revitalize the domestic equities ecosystem, says Thilan Wickramasinghe, Maybank Securities head of research, in a note. "Of course, this is no silver bullet especially in the context of rising competition from regional exchanges and the revival of Hong Kong/China," he writes. However, the measures could contribute to boosting liquidity and valuations, as well as broadening market participation, while supporting investor safeguards, he says. Maybank expects Singapore Exchange to benefit from higher trading and listing fees. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0602 GMT - DLF is likely to benefit from planned property launches of over INR1 trillion, Ambit Capital analysts say in a research report. The real-estate developer should deliver cumulated pre-sales of INR764 billion over FY 2025-2027, driven by these launches, the analysts estimate. The Indian company's re-entry into Mumbai includes a 2.3-million square feet project with revenue potential of INR58 billion, the analysts note. Success in this project could facilitate DLF's expansion in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, India's largest market by value, the analysts add. The brokerage initiates coverage of the stock with a buy rating and a target price of INR900.00. Shares are 1.4% lower at INR676.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0512 GMT - Ratchthani Leasing appears set to enter an asset-quality recovery cycle, according to Thanachart Securities' Sarachada Sornsong in a research report, as the brokerage reaffirms its buy rating on the stock. The Thai company has taken a more cautious approach to new lending and strengthened its debt collection processes since 2Q 2023, bringing its balance sheet clean-up to near completion, the analyst notes. The financial services provider is expected to maintain its prudent stance, prioritizing loan quality over aggressive portfolio expansion. However, the brokerage lowers its 2025-2027 earnings estimates for Ratchthani Leasing by average 52%, citing factors such as elevated provisions. The target price is reduced to THB1.84 from THB2.64. Shares are 1.2% higher at THB1.66. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: CANACCORD GENUITY GROUP INC. THROUGH ITS WEALTH MANAGEMENT BUSINESS IN THE UK & CROWN DEPENDENCIES ANNOUNCES COMPLETION OF ITS ACQUISITION OF BROOKS MACDONALD ASSET MANAGEMENT (INTERNATIONAL) LTD.
data.description:
CANACCORD GENUITY GROUP INC. THROUGH ITS WEALTH MANAGEMENT BUSINESS IN THE UK & CROWN DEPENDENCIES ANNOUNCES COMPLETION OF ITS ACQUISITION OF BROOKS MACDONALD ASSET MANAGEMENT (INTERNATIONAL) LTD.

Canada NewsWire

TORONTO, February 24, 2025

TORONTO, February 24, 2025 /CNW/ - Canaccord Genuity Group Inc. (the "Company") is pleased to announce that through its wealth management business in the UK & Crown Dependencies ("CGWM UK") it has completed its purchase of Brooks Macdonald Asset Management (International) Limited ("BMI"), previously a wholly owned subsidiary of Brooks Macdonald Group plc ("Brooks Macdonald").

The acquisition represents an important strategic addition to the Company's operations in Guernsey, the Isle of Man, and Jersey. As previously disclosed, the professionals and clients of BMI will benefit from being part of a larger integrated wealth management business with deep resources and a strong commitment to supporting their long-term growth and success.

David Esfandi, Chief Executive Officer of CGWM UK said: "This acquisition is another step forward in our mission to become the best integrated wealth manager in the region by performance and service. With this investment, we increase our ability to serve the complex needs of international clients who have clear ambitions and need a trusted partner to help them achieve their goals."

ABOUT CANACCORD GENUITY GROUP INC.

Through its principal subsidiaries, Canaccord Genuity Group Inc. (the "Company") is a leading independent, full-service financial services firm, with operations in two principal segments of the securities industry: wealth management and capital markets. Since its establishment in 1950, the Company has been driven by an unwavering commitment to building lasting client relationships. We achieve this by generating value for our individual, institutional and corporate clients through comprehensive investment solutions, brokerage services and investment banking services. The Company has wealth management offices located in Canada, the UK, Guernsey, Jersey, the Isle of Man and Australia. The Company's international capital markets division operates in North America, UK & Europe, Asia, and Australia.

Canaccord Genuity Group Inc. is publicly traded under the symbol CF on the TSX.

CAUTION REGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

In evaluating these statements, readers should specifically consider various factors that may cause actual results to differ materially from any forward-looking statement. data.title: Samsung Electronics Agrees on 5.1% Wage Increase for 2025
data.description:
By Kwanwoo Jun

Samsung Electronics' management and union negotiators have agreed on a 5.1% wage increase for this year.

Each Samsung employee will also receive 30 stocks and a two-million-point online shopping reward, which can be used to purchase Samsung goods, as perks under the preliminary deal, the South Korean technology company said in a statement Monday.

Members of the National Samsung Electronics Union, which currently claims to have around 36,000 members, will vote on the deal for approval by March 5, the company said.

Management and union representatives will also launch a joint task force to enhance the bonus system, it added.

Unionized Samsung workers last year launched their first-ever walkout to press for higher wages and other demands. The labor unrest, however, caused no major disruptions to highly automated chip-making productions.

Write to Kwanwoo Jun at kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com

data.title: Singapore Airlines Fair Value Estimate Raised to S$6.50 From S$6.30 by OCBC Investment Research >C6L.SG
Ningbo Energy Group Establishes New Subsidiary for Solar Power Project
data.description:
Ningbo Energy Group subsidiary Langchen New Energy invested in the establishment of wholly owned Ma'anshan Langchen.

Ma'anshan Langchen, which has a registered capital of 5 million yuan, aims to develop photovoltaic power generation projects as part of Ningbo Energy's strategy to expand its renewable energy sector and accelerate its green energy transition, according to a Feb. 22 filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the steam and electricity generation company rose 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Lenovo's Revenue Should Get Boost From Premium PC Offerings — Market Talk
data.description: Lenovo's PC volumes should stabilize in calendar 2025, supported by an accelerated Windows 11 replacement cycle, CGS International analyst Ray Kwok says in a note. He projects Lenovo's PC revenue to rise 1% and 3% for FY 2025 and FY 2026, respectively, as average selling prices rise due to better product mix and bigger contributions by premium PCs, such as gaming PCs and AI PCs. CGS Interational raises its FY 2026 and FY 2027 EPS forecasts on Lenovo by 13%-15% as the global PC market recovery drives demand for AI PCs and servers, supporting profitability. The brokerage raises the stock's target to HK$15.00 from HK$13.30 and maintains an add rating. Shares are last at HK$13.02. (hoishan.chan@wsj.com)

data.title: Singapore to Launch SG$5 Billion Program to Revitalize Stock Market
data.description:
The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has outlined plans to launch a SG$5 billion program to help boost the competitiveness of the city-state's equities market, according to a media release on Friday.

Through the program, the financial regulator will invest with selected fund managers capable of executing investment mandates focused on Singapore stocks.

"These strategies should be actively managed, invest in a range of companies and not just index component stocks, and over time draw in investments from other investors," MAS said in the release.

The Equity Market Development Program will be funded by MAS' investment portfolio and the financial sector development fund.

data.title: Turkish Business Morale Highest in 8 Months
data.description:
The manufacturing confidence index in Turkey rose to 102.4 in February 2025, up from 100.9 in the previous month.

This marked the highest reading since last June.

data.title: Austal Fiscal H1 Results Solid, Poised for 'Very Bright' Medium, Long term, Euroz Harlteys Says
data.description:
Austal fiscal first half results were solid, supported by an unexpected rise in revenue and earnings before interest and tax, positioning the company for a "very bright" medium and long-term outlook, according to a February 21 note by Euroz Hartley.

The company reported fiscal first-half earnings of AU$ 0.069 per diluted share, up from AU$ 0.033 per diluted share a year earlier. Analysts polled by Visible Alpha expected AU$0.066.

Revenue for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$825.7 million, up from AU$717.7 million a year earlier. Analysts surveyed by Visible Alpha expected AU$883.8 million.

Euroz noted that Austal's orderbook has grown to AU$14.2 billion, including options, and expects that a recent commercial ferry win will boost volume in its Asian shipyards, while progress on the strategic ship-building agreement adds further growth potential.

Despite about $750 million remaining in expenditure, Austal has already secured half of the required funding through the submarine module contract, with most of the cash already received, Euroz added.

Additionally, the company has a letter of support from the Australian Government for up to 50% of $300 million for its Final Assembly 2 project, leaving only $150 million to fund, the investment firm said.

Euroz believes that the total sum of opportunities supports Austal becoming an over $3 billion revenue business over the next decade once fully ramped up.

The results have boosted investor confidence in Austal's ability to fund and execute its plans, driving an upward movement in its share price.

Euroz maintained the firm's buy ratings and its price target of AU$4.01.

data.title: Gohigh Networks Ends Partnership With Zhongshen Asia Pacific Accounting Firm
data.description:
Gohigh Networks ended its partnership with Zhongshen Asia Pacific Accounting Firm, according to a Feb. 21 disclosure with the Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

Zhongshen Asia Pacific Accounting Firm was previously re-hired to do the 2024 audit, but no agreement was signed as the accounting firm would not be able to finish the audit in time due to its personnel and time arrangement, the Chinese communications equipment manufacturer said.

The company said it will appoint a new accounting firm as soon as possible.

The company's shares closed 5% lower.

data.title: Kosmos Energy Reports Lower FY24 Net Income, Total Revenue
data.description:
Kosmos Energy said Monday its net income and total revenue declined year over year in 2024.

Net income for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was $189.9 million, compared with $213.5 million earlier. EPS moved to $0.40 from $0.44.

The upstream oil company's total revenue and other income was $1.68 billion, compared with $1.70 billion a year ago.

data.title: Sumou Real Estate Wins New Infrastructure Development Project in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Sumou Real Estate Co. (SASE:4323) was awarded an infrastructure development project for Sharia Grant Scheme No. 17 from Holy Makkah Municipality in Saudi Arabia.

The real estate company will receive 7.04% of the project's sales after its development, according to a Monday filing.

Sumou said it is following up with the municipality for approval and the signing of the contract.

data.title: Just Eat Takeaway Pretax Loss Narrows, Revenue Misses Market Expectations
data.description:
(06:09 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Adjusted Ebitda EUR460M
(06:10 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Rev EUR5.085B
(06:11 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Revenue Less Adjusted Order-Fulfilment Costs EUR2.42B
(06:11 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Total Orders 879M
(06:11 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Total Gross Transaction Value EUR26.3B
(06:13 GMT) *Correct: Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Rev EUR3.56B
(06:13 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Net Loss EUR1.64B
(06:14 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Oper Loss EUR520M
(06:14 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Pretax Loss EUR532M
(06:15 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com Sees 2025 Constant Currency Gross Transaction Value Growth at 4%-8%
(06:16 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com Sees 2025 Adjusted Ebitda at EUR360M-EUR380M
(06:17 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com Targets Adjusted Ebitda Margin of 5% of Gross Transaction Value
(07:21 GMT) Just Eat Takeaway Pretax Loss Narrows, Revenue Misses Market Expectations

By Najat Kantouar

Just Eat Takeaway.com reported a narrowed pretax loss and slightly higher revenue that missed market expectations.

The Amsterdam-headquartered food-delivery group posted a pretax loss for last year of 1.645 billion euros, equivalent to $1.72 billion, compared with a loss of 1.85 billion euros a year earlier. This was driven mainly driven by noncash impairment losses.

Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization rose to 460 million euros from 339 million euros, due to an improvement in fulfillment costs per order and efficiencies in marketing, it said.

Revenue increased slightly to 3.56 billion euros from 3.53 billion euros, though was below analyst consensus of 5.16 billion euros taken from FactSet.

Gross transaction value--a key metric for the industry, representing the value of merchandise sold over a certain period--for the year declined 2% in constant currency to 26.3 billion euros. North America GTV in constant currency fell 9%, while Northern Europe grew 4%. U.K. and Ireland grew 4%. The Southern Europe, Australia and New Zealand segment declined 11%.

The company processed 879 million orders last year, down from 926 million a year prior.

For 2025, Just Eat expects constant currency GTV growth--excluding the rest of the world segment--in the range of 4% to 8% on year, with adjusted Ebitda between 360 million and 380 million euros. It also anticipates free cash flow of approximately 100 million euros.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: GSK Closes Up to $1 Billion Purchase of US-based IDRx
data.description:
GSK completed the acquisition of US-based clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company IDRx for a total cash consideration of up to $1.15 billion.

The target company is focused on developing precision therapeutics to treat gastrointestinal stromal tumors.

The pharmaceutical giant made an upfront payment of $1 billion, while the remaining amount is conditional on the success of regulatory approval. GSK added that it will also be responsible for success-based milestone payments and tiered royalties for IDRx's lead molecule IDRX-42 owed to Merck KGaA .

data.title: Market Chatter: Henderson Land Projects See High Demand
data.description:
Henderson Land Development's phase 2 of the Belgravia Place project in Cheung Sha Wan, Hong Kong received 350 orders for its 50 units, making the project oversubscribed seven times, The Standard reported Sunday.

The developer launched the price list with an average discounted price of HK$15,968 per square foot, up 0.4% than the HK$15,902 per square foot in phase 1, the report said.

Henderson also received 800 orders for 63 units at its Eight Southpark project in Kowloon City, Hong Kong, making the project 13 times oversubscribed, the report said.

The units were priced at an average of HK$17,388 per square foot, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Poland - Factors to Watch Feb 24
data.description:
The following are significant news stories, press reports and events on Monday. ALL TIMES GMT (Poland: GMT + 1 hour):

NATIONAL SECURITY COUNCIL

The National Security Council will meet at 1230 GMT to discuss the situation in Ukraine.

PRESIDENT DUDA

U.S. presence in Poland and Central Europe should be boosted, Polish President Andrzej Duda told U.S. President Donald Trump during a short meeting in Washington on Saturday.

INTEREST RATES

If rate cuts in Poland will happen at all in 2025, then they will be at the end of the year and the scale of monetary policy easing would be minor, rate-setter Iwona Duda said in an interview for PAP.

RETAIL SALES

Statistics office will release Polish retail sales data for January at 0900. Analysts polled by Reuters estimate that sales rose 1.5% year-on-year.

UKRAINIAN COMPANIES IN POLAND

In 2022-2024, almost 78,000 companies run by Ukrainians were registered in Poland. Last year was a record, when 33,200 were established, according to a report by the Polish Economic Institute published by Rzeczpospolita daily.

****Reuters has not verified stories reported by Polish media and does not vouch for their accuracy.****

data.title: India's Glenmark Pharma rises on settling anti-trust lawsuit with plaintiffs
data.description:
** Shares of Glenmark Pharmaceuticals up ~4% to 1350.5 rupees

** Co settles remaining antitrust and consumer protection lawsuits with three plaintiffs in connection with generic cholesterol drug Zetia

** Plaintiffs include Humana , Centene Corp

and Kaiser Foundation Health Plan [RIC:RIC:KISERF.UL]

** Under terms of $7 mln settlement, Glenmark denies allegations by plaintiffs, concedes no liability

** Including session's gains, GLEN shares up 48.3% in the last 12 months
